title: South Korea as a Current Institutional Proof Point
subtitle: National dictionary publication, current institutional use, and why Korea matters now
summary: Current institutional proof-point anchor for the Sutton SignWriting Evidence and Adoption series.
synopsis: This entry treats South Korea as a current institutional proof point for SignWriting. It keeps the claim disciplined: Korea is not the broad empirical center that Brazil is, but it does show that SignWriting can function inside current public dictionary infrastructure connected to a national-language institution.
